meta.title: A→B gate for the useVibe().can prompt flip — RERUN on the corrected prompt
meta.promptUnderTest: main after the requireAccess-vs-public + allowAnonymous fix (PR #2526, commit 4099c30); confirmed deployed via `vibes-diy generate --dry-run --transcript`.
meta.method: Re-ran the same 7-row matrix with new slugs (eval2525r-*). Pulled App.jsx + access.js, graded against the can-flip rule. unknown-rate by static analysis of access-runner.ts (no async, all exports extractable & db-matched, ctx.requireAccess/requireRole implemented).
meta.generationReliability: Poor this round: of 9 generate attempts, 4 push-failed server-side (Apps insert error) and 1 lint-failed (no export default). recipe-journal, team-board, photo-wall were recovered from local snapshots (content intact, gradeable) but did not deploy; forum-mods, guestbook, trip-planner, task-list deployed cleanly. The push failures are a transient server-side issue, independent of generation content.

results.accessExport: export function recipes(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: Owner-only writes gated on can.create({type:'recipe'}); edit on can.edit. Now ALSO renders the denied reason ('{reason} — browsing only.') — the prior round's missing-reason nit is fixed.

results.accessExport: export function signalBoard(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: FIXED. access.js now uses the open-channel pattern — channel doc is grant.public read; message rule checks authorHandle and routes by channelId with NO ctx.requireAccess (the access.js even carries a comment explaining public is read-only and would block non-owners). Channel create gated on can.create({type:'channel'}) with reason; message compose gated on can.create({type:'message', channelId, authorHandle}) with reason. Any signed-in user can post.

results.accessExport: export function forum(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: Posting is correct (posts/replies check authorHandle, route to a public channel, NO requireAccess — open pattern applied). BUT the moderator-appointment flow is broken: the `moderator` doc returns { members: { moderator: [handle] } } with NO channels, which the server rejects as an 'unreadable write' (app-documents-write-eventos.ts:366 / isReadableResult) and which makes can.create({type:'moderator'}) return ok:false 'unreadable write'. The appoint form is gated on canAppoint.ok, so even the owner sees 'Owner-only.' and can never appoint a moderator. Root cause is a NEW prompt bug, not the can-flip: the prompt's roleGrant/survey-config examples return grant/member docs without a channel. (Prior round happened to include channels:['forum'] on the grant doc; this is generation variance over a buggy pattern.)
results.newBug: grant/member doc returned with no channel → 'unreadable write' rejection; appoint UI hidden from the owner.

results.accessExport: export function guestbook(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: FIXED. access.js no longer throws on !user; the entry rule returns { channels:['public'], grant:{public:['public']}, allowAnonymous: true } with a comment explaining the footgun. can.create({type:'entry'}) is ok for an anonymous viewer, so anyone can sign; denied state renders reason. The exact allowAnonymous example added to fireproof.md transferred.

results.accessExport: export function snapwall(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: FIXED for the can-flip. Posts and comments (type 'post'/'message') check authorHandle and route to channelId with NO ctx.requireAccess — the open-channel pattern, with an explanatory comment. Post and comment surfaces gate on can.create({..., channelId, authorHandle}) with reason. Channel creation is now gated on can.create({type:'channel', _id}) (no more useViewer().isOwner write-gate). The canonical comment flow works for any signed-in viewer.
nits: Author-preservation gap (Codex P2, separate from the can-flip): the post AND message rules check only doc.authorHandle, not oldDoc.authorHandle, so on an update a signed-in user can PUT over another user's doc _id and reassign authorHandle to themselves. team-board/task-list/forum guard this with an `if (oldDoc && oldDoc.authorHandle !== user.userHandle) throw`; photo-wall does not. Doesn't change the can-flip PASS, but it's a real generated-access.js correctness gap.

results.accessExport: export default function (doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: can-flip wiring is correct (trip/activity/packing gate on can.create with reason; per-row delete/edit gated). But the access model is broken: activity/packing writes call ctx.requireAccess('trip'), and the only grants on 'trip' are grant.public (read-only) + grant.roles.planner — and NO ONE is ever made a planner (the roleGrant doc returns { members } with no channel → unreadable write, AND the App.jsx surfaces no grant UI). So requireAccess('trip') is never satisfied outside admin mode: no one (not even the owner, without the admin toggle) can add an activity or packing item. This is the SAME requireAccess-vs-membership trap, reached via a role that's never granted. Prior round's trip used an open any-author pattern and passed; the corrected prompt's role examples nudged it into a restricted model it can't wire up.
results.newBug: requireAccess on a channel whose writers hold only public read + an ungranted role; roleGrant doc unreadable (no channel) and no grant UI → writes blocked for everyone in normal mode.
nits: Also has the author-preservation gap: activity/packing check only doc.authorHandle, not oldDoc.authorHandle (moot here since writes are already blocked, but the pattern is present).

results.accessExport: export function tasks(doc, oldDoc, user, ctx)
results.reason: RECOVERED. This time the composer is real (not a stub): create gated on can.create(draft) with reason rendered, edit/delete gated on can.edit(task)/can.delete(task). Open board (any signed-in author, authorHandle check, public channel). The prior round's row-7 generation flake did not recur.

crossCuttingFindings.summary: Editable author-owned docs check only doc.authorHandle, not oldDoc.authorHandle, so a signed-in user can overwrite another user's doc _id and reassign authorship. Present in photo-wall (post, message) and trip-planner (activity, packing); correctly guarded in team-board, task-list, forum, guestbook (write-once). A candidate 3rd prompt tweak-spot: teach 'on update, also verify oldDoc.authorHandle — never let an author be reassigned.'

deltaVsPriorRun: task-list generation-flake → PASS (composer generated this time)
recipe-journal PASS → PASS (now also renders reason)
forum-mods PASS → FAIL (new bug: unreadable grant doc)
trip-planner PASS → FAIL (new bug: requireAccess on ungranted-role channel)
rollup.verdict: TARGETED BUG FIXED; OPEN/ANONYMOUS APPS FLIP-READY; ROLE-MANAGEMENT NEEDS ONE MORE PROMPT FIX
rollup.verdictRationale: The bug the A→B gate was built to catch — public-read channels gated on ctx.requireAccess, silently blocking non-owner writes — is resolved. Every open-channel / anonymous row recovered: team-board, photo-wall, and guestbook all flipped to PASS, task-list recovered, and recipe-journal now renders its denied reason too. unknown rate stayed 0. The two remaining FAILs are role-management apps (forum-mods, trip-planner) and share a single NEW, isolated root cause inherited from the prompt's role examples: grant/role-grant/member docs are returned WITHOUT a channel, so the server rejects them as 'unreadable write' (announcements roleGrant + survey-config in fireproof.md model exactly this), and the role→requireAccess round-trip therefore never completes (no member is ever added, and apps surface no grant UI). Recommend a focused follow-up: (1) every grant/role-grant/member doc must also route to a channel (fix the roleGrant + survey-config examples to return a channel, e.g. an owner/admin channel); (2) make the restricted-channel worked example end-to-end — a writable grant doc plus a gated grant UI — or prefer the open-channel pattern when the prompt doesn't clearly require restriction. Open-channel and anonymous apps look ready to flip now; hold role-management apps for the follow-up fix, then re-run rows 3 and 6.
